Fellow American,

**Yesterday, the RNC announced the location of the first Presidential Debate.**

I am going to put in the work EVERY DAY to make sure I am on that debate stage.

While we don't know today the criteria to be invited to the main debate stage yet, it's clear that the number of unique grassroots donors will play a major role.

To make sure I can share my vision for America on the debate stage, _**[I need your help.](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=ed99cba4af77440abcc0558b181d2d61&_e=l-sZMpRJ_CT-6ogNhV-lAF8vVgFh3f0n2O1DI4ysArTGQjiCLvgmfywd-l4afw7eUoj54Cn8g9Li2lK13lDH41M5Z1JW4Do5ProTqCiGx5KOrEYTwL1tGuSXHzYwXFXkUVx5YCilI3mVlKDDW2lg1vCy3woDexoOHp3fG-W2cHSdXRwSmrvi3cFRmqq5_srQ7w7EBR77svSbJ2O8bGLb8IySMjp1QOpKCfNoMYpaDHNS_Nuaugg9s6jvE3OpBFqcwLmb5VvaTzP6qDeRx4SmkWwPC8WTnOoIP3gm2jc6yNK44qFNpdPqSpIQpu7siFWz4HubHRYgwAtt69hiTJuP0H38y_MVGYdUumlF63bE4Kq_VBAe0RnKwlMrnbG3j1hFDb9l2Ekfg4nkmQwVh-pTgKVI4djVg0coKwJ8qYuEqjI5Utz4lXDwE5GBkow3pcw2)**_

**GET TO KNOW VIVEK:** Vivek is an American entrepreneur, political and cultural thought leader, and New York Times bestselling author of **_Woke, Inc.: Inside Corporate America's Social Justice Scam_** , along with his second book, **_Nation of Victims: Identity Politics, the Death of Merit, and the Path Back to Excellence_**.

He has been a prominent and outspoken critic against ESG, discriminatory practices under the guise of critical race theory, and of what he calls "government-tech censorship," where the government colludes with big tech to do through the backdoor what it couldn't do through the front door under the Constitution.

Vivek is a first-generation American. Growing up in the Cincinnati area, he often recounts the sage advice he received from his father that "If you're going to stand out, then you might as well be outstanding." This advice set the course for his life: an accomplished pianist, a nationally ranked tennis player, and the valedictorian of his Jesuit high school. He went on to graduate summa cum laude in Biology from Harvard in 2007 and received his J.D. degree from Yale Law School in 2013, worked at a hedge fund, then started a pharmaceutical company, Roivant Sciences, where his team developed five drugs that went on to become FDA-approved.

In 2022, he founded Strive Asset Management, an Ohio-based firm, that takes direct aim at asset managers like BlackRock, State Street, Vanguard and others for their fiduciary breach of duty to maximize the wealth of their clients for Wokeism. Strive's mission is to restore the voices of everyday citizens in the American economy by leading companies to focus on excellence over politics.

Breaking news, Peter: | | **“Justice Department to take abortion pill fight to Supreme Court”**
_— _ABC News_ , 4/13/23_
* * *
| [SUPPORT ABORTION ACCESS](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/61768831/401113900/-253429511?refcode=AU_HTP_FR_GEN_RES_AC_20230413_PM2_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS8xMDI1NDMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZTZkNWZjYjItNThkYS1lZDExLThlOGItMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=IPAUaR9lVN1kMyD0XkYx-axi1TvU5cG1R4ym_ZCUCU4=)

Late last night, an appeals court ruled that mifepristone could stay on the market, but access would be severely restricted — patients would no longer be able to receive the medication by mail.
Mailing abortion pills was a lifeline for women in states where abortion is banned or the nearest care is too far away.
That’s why Attorney General Merrick Garland just announced the Department of Justice is planning to bring this fight to the Supreme Court. We need all hands on deck, so we set an emergency goal to raise $5,000 by Saturday night in order to fight back. That’s why we’re asking:
[_**Can you rush an urgent contribution of any amount to help us hit our emergency goal? There’s so much at stake and we can’t afford to fall short now:**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/61768832/401113901/-253429511?refcode=AU_HTP_FR_GEN_RES_AC_20230413_PM2_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS8xMDI1NDMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZTZkNWZjYjItNThkYS1lZDExLThlOGItMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=IPAUaR9lVN1kMyD0XkYx-axi1TvU5cG1R4ym_ZCUCU4=)

clean_body: | = Breaking news, Peter: | | “Justice Department to take abortion pill fight to Supreme Court” — ABC News , 4/13/23 | = Late last night, an appeals court ruled that mifepristone could stay on the market, but access would be severely restricted — patients would no longer be able to receive the medication by mail. Mailing abortion pills was a lifeline for women in states where abortion is banned or the nearest care is too far away. That’s why Attorney General Merrick Garland just announced the Department of Justice is planning to bring this fight to the Supreme Court. We need all hands on deck, so we set an emergency goal to raise $5,000 by Saturday night in order to fight back. name: Fair Fight Action
subject: Voter suppression laws have no place in our democracy
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FAIR FIGHT ACTION
body:
As you read this, anti-voter attacks are on the rise in every corner of the country. |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/61768859/401115446/1793776944?refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20230413_f_anti_v1s2&ms=bsc_emm_ffa_20230413_f_anti_v1s2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDE0YTExOS01OWRhLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_8TRs0quhkfKuGPboZblPSbCvUVII8V8BTNLGGH6_wc=&emci=6f61857b-3cda-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&emdi=2014a119-59da-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&ceid=1037277)

| | As you read this, anti-voter attacks are on the rise in every corner of the country. From Georgia to Hawaii, legislators in 32 states have proposed laws to suppress the freedom to vote. It’s pretty simple, but bears repeating in this moment: Our elected leaders should protect — not restrict — the freedom to vote. [_**We need to know that voting rights champions around the country are paying attention and are on our side. Sign your name if you agree that this dangerous anti-voter movement has no place in our democracy.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/61768860/401115447/-1304620791?refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20230413_f_anti_v1s2&ms=bsc_emm_ffa_20230413_f_anti_v1s2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDE0YTExOS01OWRhLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_8TRs0quhkfKuGPboZblPSbCvUVII8V8BTNLGGH6_wc=&emci=6f61857b-3cda-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&emdi=2014a119-59da-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&ceid=1037277) | [SIGN YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/61768861/401115448/-1304620791?refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20230413_f_anti_v1s2&ms=bsc_emm_ffa_20230413_f_anti_v1s2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDE0YTExOS01OWRhLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_8TRs0quhkfKuGPboZblPSbCvUVII8V8BTNLGGH6_wc=&emci=6f61857b-3cda-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&emdi=2014a119-59da-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&ceid=1037277)
---
Across the country, anti-democratic legislators are introducing harmful bills aimed to further restrict the freedom to vote. From implementing burdensome voter ID requirements to reducing the number of polling locations and hours, this coordinated legislative attack is designed to make it harder for people to cast their ballots and raise their voices.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/61768862/401115449/-1304620791?refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20230413_f_anti_v1s2&ms=bsc_emm_ffa_20230413_f_anti_v1s2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDE0YTExOS01OWRhLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_8TRs0quhkfKuGPboZblPSbCvUVII8V8BTNLGGH6_wc=&emci=6f61857b-3cda-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&emdi=2014a119-59da-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&ceid=1037277)
The Fair Fight Action team is fighting back. We’re not just on the lookout for voter suppression legislation — we’ve been working with pro-democracy leaders to propose bills that expand voting rights, and we’ve been organizing with activists to oppose voter suppression legislation in Georgia and alongside our partners nationwide. The reality is this: We need to be ready to respond swiftly and effectively to attempts to undermine free and fair elections.
There’s no off-season when it comes to protecting voting rights — so we need to strengthen our movement and raise our voices now to stop voter suppression attempts before they restrict access to the ballot box in upcoming elections.
[_**Please sign your name right away to say no to voter suppression laws wherever and whenever they emerge >>**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/61768863/401115450/-1304620791?refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20230413_f_anti_v1s2&ms=bsc_emm_ffa_20230413_f_anti_v1s2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDE0YTExOS01OWRhLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_8TRs0quhkfKuGPboZblPSbCvUVII8V8BTNLGGH6_wc=&emci=6f61857b-3cda-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&emdi=2014a119-59da-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&ceid=1037277)
[SIGN YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/61768864/401115451/-1304620791?refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20230413_f_anti_v1s2&ms=bsc_emm_ffa_20230413_f_anti_v1s2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDE0YTExOS01OWRhLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_8TRs0quhkfKuGPboZblPSbCvUVII8V8BTNLGGH6_wc=&emci=6f61857b-3cda-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&emdi=2014a119-59da-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&ceid=1037277)

Derek,

The YDA Issue Advocacy Committee is excited to announce a free virtual training series to help prepare young Democrats to make their voices heard — join Issue Advocacy in partnership with the YDA Legislation Committee for a two-part training series led by the **DNC****Best Practices Institute**. This is an event that you don't want to miss:

name: Tammy Baldwin
subject: I’m officially running for reelection to the United States Senate
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ron Be Gone, Inc
body:
| Hi there — I have exciting news to share, and I wanted you to hear it from me:

**I’m officially running for reelection to the United States Senate.**

I know the road ahead won’t always be easy — election experts are already calling me one of the most vulnerable Senate Democrats on the ballot next year. But with you by my side, I know we can get this done and win big next November.

[**So, will you help me start my reelection in the strongest position by splitting a donation between my campaign in Wisconsin and Ron Be Gone today?**](https://act.ronbegone.com/go/16344?t=7&akid=1758%2E564421%2E0i4tCJ) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Ron Be Gone and Tammy Baldwin will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $3 »](https://act.ronbegone.com/go/16350?t=13&akid=1758%2E564421%2E0i4tCJ)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $7 »](https://act.ronbegone.com/go/16351?t=14&akid=1758%2E564421%2E0i4tCJ)
[Or a custom donation »](https://act.ronbegone.com/go/16352?t=15&akid=1758%2E564421%2E0i4tCJ)
I’m so proud of the progress we’ve made in the Senate over the past few years, Friend. We’ve lowered the price of prescription drugs, addressed the climate crisis with landmark investment in clean energy, expanded health care for millions of our veterans, and so much more.

A highlight of my time in the Senate was when the _Respect for Marriage Act_ , my bipartisan bill to protect marriage equality, was signed into law late last year. That’s a moment I will never forget.

But there’s a lot more to be done, and I’m not willing to give up the fight to make things easier, better, safer, and stronger for hard-working Wisconsinites.

We have to protect Social Security and Medicare for millions of hard-working seniors who deserve the benefits they’ve spent their lives working towards.

We need to defend voting rights and protect access to the ballot box — we’ve seen the harmful impacts of right-wing extremists who try to deny election results and take away Americans’ right to safely and securely participate in elections.

And we need to re-establish protections for abortion access. After _Roe v. Wade_ was overturned, I was devastated and angry, and I’m sure you were too. Wisconsin just took a huge first step in the right direction with our Supreme Court race last week, but we still have a long way to go toward making sure every woman has the freedom to make her own decisions no matter what zip code she lives in.

I remain hopeful that we can do all of this — and more — together. But it won’t be easy, especially if Republicans have it their way. Their views are becoming more out-of-touch and dangerous by the day, and I know for sure that they will come after my Senate seat with everything they’ve got. They’re already spending big on misleading ads attacking my record.

**But with you by my side, I know we can win. We’ve done it before, together, and I wouldn’t want to have it any other way this time around.**

[**Will you stand by me again and split a contribution between my reelection campaign and Ron Be Gone? It’d mean the world to me to have you with me from Day One.**](https://act.ronbegone.com/go/16344?t=17&akid=1758%2E564421%2E0i4tCJ)
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Ron Be Gone and Tammy Baldwin will go through immediately:
[CONTRIBUTE $3 »](https://act.ronbegone.com/go/16350?t=23&akid=1758%2E564421%2E0i4tCJ)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $7 »](https://act.ronbegone.com/go/16351?t=24&akid=1758%2E564421%2E0i4tCJ)
[Or a custom donation »](https://act.ronbegone.com/go/16352?t=25&akid=1758%2E564421%2E0i4tCJ)
Thank you so much, folks. I can’t wait to see you on the campaign trail, and I promise I’ll keep you updated on every step of this journey. So excited for what’s in store for us.

I’m so glad to have you on this historic team.

Let’s do this.

— Tammy
| | Paid for by Ron Be Gone, Inc.
(www.ronbegone.com)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. P.O. Box 30295, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33303

MAGA Republican Gov. Ron DeSantis is the worst Governor in America and an utter nightmare for Floridians. He is relentless in his praise for Donald Trump, he completely botched Florida's pandemic response and put lives at risk, and he gave scarce COVID vaccines to his major campaign donors instead of the most vulnerable Floridians. He MUST be defeated in the next election. We know for sure that Donald Trump is going to go ALL OUT to help DeSantis win, so we need to start fighting back today by building a massive coalition of good Democratic supporters.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution to beat Ron DeSantis by clicking here](https://act.ronbegone.com/go/118?t=1&akid=1758%2E564421%2E0i4tCJ). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and ensure that we can implement our entire strategy to crush Ron DeSantis, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.ronbegone.com/go/119?t=2&akid=1758%2E564421%2E0i4tCJ).**

name: Nancy Mace
subject: Read this to understand why I keep emailing you

Peter,

I know you probably receive a lot of fundraising emails in your inbox, **so I wanted to take a second to say thank you for reading this one and explain why your continued support is so critical.**

[**Running a campaign like this one is _extremely_ expensive**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=0423f5ab0ef9401fb31ccf33e78cd180&_e=vLovdnYwv8S4ZEc7-S4ZHyjwqkENkhjewJ9de24vvM35cXXLIxBv5NO_O9cUNrw7RthjqSz2FQxq7isRMC7pTAtvIjgyPqDOQQwuhKCCl5WPlvzgVjs4fGHd5GwhCMsLLDv7bLw1qXRssUQDynnomiX-IgY-8DbzFKjo1WQS402TOGTi9pzt17jYPM2RADPQ4IrRvJ8ExxuVHXQijVPbbyC50WRklAmoupKNZss8Lb9dmcsxkyrWRPTgRIgBxh-HgRJfZsSWSwYbNAbs9Aie8RJAyiKWdZiDqiJlUKFOhekzqR-3x_ITqe8XlsXpKDsjPwKoLhBorYxsZyARPnHJYJmsxVWqC5gYOQGlUBYWG4pP8bjTXOTUktHD37S-CPND3tUwEDQKOCEglNTad--P2A%3D%3D) – especially with the attacks coming at me from all sides of the Democratic party.

Just take a moment to think about all the things which go into a campaign for Congress:

* Paying our staff
* Door-knocking software
* Billboard advertising
* Get-out-the-vote efforts
* Travel expenses for the team
* Pizza and coffee for volunteers
* Digital advertisements, and emails just like this one!

It really starts to add up... _and do you know how most politicians pay for it?_ They come from wealthy families!

Well Peter, it’s not the case for me. I pulled myself up by my bootstraps – after dropping out of high school and taking classes at a technical college to graduate, then going on to graduate from the Citadel and working my way to Congress to represent real people like you and me.

I didn’t grow up with a silver spoon in my mouth. I got by without government handouts or radical socialist policies – and I am a better woman because of it. [**So, to stay in this fight I have to build a grassroots-funded warchest – meaning donations from folks just like you.**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=0423f5ab0ef9401fb31ccf33e78cd180&_e=vLovdnYwv8S4ZEc7-S4ZHyjwqkENkhjewJ9de24vvM35cXXLIxBv5NO_O9cUNrw7RthjqSz2FQxq7isRMC7pTAtvIjgyPqDOQQwuhKCCl5WPlvzgVjs4fGHd5GwhCMsLLDv7bLw1qXRssUQDynnomiX-IgY-8DbzFKjo1WQS402TOGTi9pzt17jYPM2RADPQ4IrRvJ8ExxuVHXQijVPbbyC50WRklAmoupKNZss8Lb9qxki2nni61MLQc8cyG7WLZ3gFDl54UqJ2sh9iCD3E7qodX3hsu42w9esv-Yyp3O8uZlyBWHlNs8sFVWXw8PiMat-u2y4ayX9J_fVlFkzr1ZEwh5UTDEYHSLWUneu8SzmaT_xYk6aJi0e9etE9DS7-vkI5obolqcpjfOT1Bi3jJA%3D%3D)

Do you know why Washington is filled to the brim with wealthy folks who think they can buy seats? [**Because they have the funds to dump millions into ultra-competitive seats just like this one.**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=0423f5ab0ef9401fb31ccf33e78cd180&_e=vLovdnYwv8S4ZEc7-S4ZHyjwqkENkhjewJ9de24vvM35cXXLIxBv5NO_O9cUNrw7RthjqSz2FQxq7isRMC7pTAtvIjgyPqDOQQwuhKCCl5WPlvzgVjs4fGHd5GwhCMsLLDv7bLw1qXRssUQDynnomiX-IgY-8DbzFKjo1WQS402TOGTi9pzt17jYPM2RADPQ4IrRvJ8ExxuVHXQijVPbbyC50WRklAmoupKNZss8Lb-UmYTyAozamNjdM170UCjofLyPtcfPxudi4W1fQFuS6pNd6iKA3N7klILPXNLtaTFSvnn2EnryF4R1bDVWR_AkFtaowFOzBVehH2__-xIRQwYSi4phQGH-zEsjtZrWp21M81MBqtHv7gyFiyFeZsCaOI5f40GeM5LtnapmVa-mPA%3D%3D)

# About Nancy
---
Congresswoman Nancy Mace grew up in the Lowcountry. She is the daughter of a retired Army General and retired school teacher. She is a proud mother of two, the first female to graduate from The Citadel's Corps of Cadets, and a tough conservative who wants to save our country from the radicals who hate America! In 2020, she took SC-01 back from the Democrats and now, is fighting tooth and nail to keep it Red. [**JOIN NANCY**](go.txting.io/mEWDMy)

Frankly, **[I’m going to ask you to pitch in toward my campaign’s mid-month goal in just a moment.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/61769072/401122641/1960715033?amount=3&refcode=AU_MTH_FR_GEN_MMG_AC_20230413_PM1_V2_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZGRlOGJmNi01YmRhLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EOU9nBUSEJHVc-5HOfvWZaoHA2WKnNvLgVl-PGrRBl0=)** But first, I hope you’ll let me explain why your support is so important — especially right now. The _only_ thing stopping Republicans from enacting their extreme, out-of-touch agenda is our Democratic Senate Majority. As House Republicans threaten to cut critical benefits like Medicaid and SNAP (formerly known as food stamps), Democrats are fighting in the Senate to expand these programs. Following a Trump-appointed federal judge’s partisan ruling to suspend safe, FDA-approved abortion medication, we’re committed to continuing to confirm judges nominated by President Biden who put science before politics. I work every day to deliver results for New Mexicans and folks across the country. And you can play a key role in keeping me and Senate Democrats in the majority, where we are in the best position to counter the GOP’s extreme policies and build on our progress. That’s why my team has reached out to ask for support for battleground Senate Democrats like Jacky Rosen in Nevada, Bob Casey in Pennsylvania, and Tammy Baldwin in Wisconsin. Today, I’m asking you to help defend my seat in New Mexico: **Can you chip in $3 or any amount ahead of my campaign’s mid-month deadline? clean_body: Frankly, = But first, I hope you’ll let me explain why your support is so important — especially right now. The only thing stopping Republicans from enacting their extreme, out-of-touch agenda is our Democratic Senate Majority. As House Republicans threaten to cut critical benefits like Medicaid and SNAP (formerly known as food stamps), Democrats are fighting in the Senate to expand these programs. Following a Trump-appointed federal judge’s partisan ruling to suspend safe, FDA-approved abortion medication, we’re committed to continuing to confirm judges nominated by President Biden who put science before politics. I work every day to deliver results for New Mexicans and folks across the country. And you can play a key role in keeping me and Senate Democrats in the majority, where we are in the best position to counter the GOP’s extreme policies and build on our progress. That’s why my team has reached out to ask for support for battleground Senate Democrats like Jacky Rosen in Nevada, Bob Casey in Pennsylvania, and Tammy Baldwin in Wisconsin. Today, I’m asking you to help defend my seat in New Mexico: Can you chip in $3 or any amount ahead of my campaign’s mid-month deadline? Your support will help keep our shared values in the Senate. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Thanks for any amount you can give today. — Martin | Paid for by Martin Heinrich for Senate Senator Martin Heinrich is committed to helping New Mexico become a leader in defense, tech, and energy with opportunities in every corner. subject: Join the fight! Become a 2023 Sustaining Member Today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Washington State Republican Party
body:
What are you waiting for?

http://wsrp.org
Ballots Are Out, Vote By November 2nd!

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WeGlgXqOx-Y

With ballots out, now is the perfect time to get out there and volunteer on a conservative campaign. Every door you knock on in these local races matter a lot and the results of this election year will be used as a bellwether for the 2022 midterms.

We are also hosting another WSRP Statewide Day Of Action on Saturday, October 30th! Bring a friend or neighbor to volunteer for a few hours to help local conservatives win in November! Sign up here to join our Statewide Day Of Action! (https://form.jotform.com/212737084845058)
State Sen. Gildon On Vaccine Mandates
https://soundcloud.com/user-450836972/wagop-report-state-sen-chris-gildon-on-vaccine-mandates-and-democrat-anti-policing-laws
State Senator Chris Gildon (R - Puyallup) joins the podcast this week to discuss vaccine mandates, anti-policing laws, the Long-Term Care Tax, and how he got his start in politics. WAGOP is now on Apple Podcasts and Spotify, follow us and never miss an episode! Listen here. (https://soundcloud.com/user-450836972/wagop-report-state-sen-chris-gildon-on-vaccine-mandates-and-democrat-anti-policing-laws)
Republican Leaders Fight Inslee's Mandate
Thanks to our Republican leaders in the State House and Senate for fighting back and offering a strong rebuke of Inslee's harmful vaccine mandate. Below is an excerpt of their most recent statement to the press:

“We are already experiencing higher prices and empty shelves partially as a result of COVID's shutdowns and workforce shortages. Today's deadline, with its unnecessarily hardline consequences, will only make that worse when hundreds, if not thousands, lose their jobs and are denied unemployment benefits. It also threatens public safety by reducing our police forces at a time when crime is already rising. People feel unsafe. They are afraid of what criminals will do when there are even fewer law enforcement officers to respond. And they are worried about people's chances of survival if there aren't enough first responders to help in an emergency. The governor underestimates the potential impact of his actions.

“Coercion, intimidation, threats and public shaming are not tactics a leader should be using against the people. The governor's obvious disdain for those who are choosing to lose their jobs rather than compromise their right to make their own medical decisions is unhelpful. He is dividing the public – segregating us and turning people against each other. Yesterday, even unvaccinated health-care workers were heroes. Today, they become villains in the governor's narrative."

Read the full statement. (https://jtwilcox.houserepublicans.wa.gov/2021/10/18/republican-leaders-todays-vaccine-deadline-threatens-critical-services-public-safety/?fbclid=IwAR3WxI9H29hbbmfbZhdkDS9QjqdVd2TIRCdjNjUkUlLdeThtbUjK_WBWJuw)
Smiley Campaign Shows Grassroots Support In Latest Fundraising Quarter
Tiffany Smiley is fighting hard to retire Patty Murray and her latest fundraising numbers show not only has Tiffany raised more than $1.6 million but she also has unequivocal grassroots support, with over 10,000 donors from Washington State. Flipping a U.S. Senate seat is hard work but Tiffany has shown in the only 5 months since starting her campaign, that she is a force to be reckoned with. Learn more about how Tiffany stands on the issues here (https://www.smileyforwashington.com/issues) .

clean_body: What are you waiting for? Ballots Are Out, Vote By November 2nd! With ballots out, now is the perfect time to get out there and volunteer on a conservative campaign. Every door you knock on in these local races matter a lot and the results of this election year will be used as a bellwether for the 2022 midterms. We are also hosting another WSRP Statewide Day Of Action on Saturday, October 30th! Bring a friend or neighbor to volunteer for a few hours to help local conservatives win in November! Sign up here to join our Statewide Day Of Action! () State Sen. Gildon On Vaccine Mandates State Senator Chris Gildon (R - Puyallup) joins the podcast this week to discuss vaccine mandates, anti-policing laws, the Long-Term Care Tax, and how he got his start in politics. WAGOP is now on Apple Podcasts and Spotify, follow us and never miss an episode! Listen here. () Republican Leaders Fight Inslee's Mandate Thanks to our Republican leaders in the State House and Senate for fighting back and offering a strong rebuke of Inslee's harmful vaccine mandate. Below is an excerpt of their most recent statement to the press: “We are already experiencing higher prices and empty shelves partially as a result of COVID's shutdowns and workforce shortages. Today's deadline, with its unnecessarily hardline consequences, will only make that worse when hundreds, if not thousands, lose their jobs and are denied unemployment benefits. It also threatens public safety by reducing our police forces at a time when crime is already rising. People feel unsafe. They are afraid of what criminals will do when there are even fewer law enforcement officers to respond. And they are worried about people's chances of survival if there aren't enough first responders to help in an emergency. The governor underestimates the potential impact of his actions. “Coercion, intimidation, threats and public shaming are not tactics a leader should be using against the people. The governor's obvious disdain for those who are choosing to lose their jobs rather than compromise their right to make their own medical decisions is unhelpful. He is dividing the public – segregating us and turning people against each other. Yesterday, even unvaccinated health-care workers were heroes. Today, they become villains in the governor's narrative." Read the full statement. () Smiley Campaign Shows Grassroots Support In Latest Fundraising Quarter Tiffany Smiley is fighting hard to retire Patty Murray and her latest fundraising numbers show not only has Tiffany raised more than $1.6 million but she also has unequivocal grassroots support, with over 10,000 donors from Washington State. Flipping a U.S. Senate seat is hard work but Tiffany has shown in the only 5 months since starting her campaign, that she is a force to be reckoned with. Learn more about how Tiffany stands on the issues here () . We take our solemn duty of protecting our children seriously | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/1320e58c5742/n41kepo7ku-468946?e=25844e23a8)

---
| | Peter,
---
| Nebraska Conservatives have had a HISTORIC season of winning, and today’s victory was a MAJOR one!

Today Senator Kauth’s bill [LB 574](https://mcshanellc.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1d82dcd2e67c4075340c26b37&id=b5aaeaba11&e=25844e23a8), the “Let Them Grow Act” just passed Round 2 on the way to becoming law!

We want to share a huge shoutout of thanks to Senator Kauth and our legislative body for every effort that has resulted in this bill passing Select File!

When this bill becomes law, Nebraska’s children will be safer from the whims of transgender activists in the medical and pharmaceutical industry seeking financial gain in the mutilation of our children.
---
| [ ](https://mcshanellc.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1d82dcd2e67c4075340c26b37&id=6b50fbff76&e=25844e23a8)
---
| The Radical Left have no qualms about our children being brainwashed to maim and dismember themselves. Tonight our fellow Nebraskans across the political aisle _already sent out a call to action_ to _“End GOP Extremism in NE”. _

We know that the majority of Nebraskans see how the irreversible gender reassignment surgery for minors **is ideological extremism** – and harmful to our youth!

If you haven’t signed up to be a part of our **[2nd House Legislative Task Force](https://mcshanellc.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1d82dcd2e67c4075340c26b37&id=b99d277e70&e=25844e23a8)** , I encourage you to do so today!

**[When you chip in even $5, $10, or $25, you are helping us to fight anti-American adversaries so that we are victorious in preserving our Republican values.](https://mcshanellc.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1d82dcd2e67c4075340c26b37&id=d9084d88e8&e=25844e23a8)**

name: Shahid Buttar
subject: On the take, or asleep at the switch?
body:
Listen now (22 min) | News reports have focused on the Pentagon's embarrassment by recent leaks, overlooking how Pentagon leaks expose continuing failures by Congress and the press
| |
---|---|---
| | | [Open in app](https://open.substack.com/pub/shahidbuttar/p/on-the-take-or-asleep-at-the-switch?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store) or [online](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9zaGFoaWRidXR0YXIuc3Vic3RhY2suY29tL3Avb24tdGhlLXRha2Utb3ItYXNsZWVwLWF0LXRoZS1zd2l0Y2g_.Quh5KwGYnA0jEavqFuoRKEUEAHSpbIRma4vHytq2_j8?)

[On the take, or asleep at the switch?](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9zaGFoaWRidXR0YXIuc3Vic3RhY2suY29tL3Avb24tdGhlLXRha2Utb3ItYXNsZWVwLWF0LXRoZS1zd2l0Y2g_.Quh5KwGYnA0jEavqFuoRKEUEAHSpbIRma4vHytq2_j8?)
News reports have focused on the Pentagon's embarrassment by recent leaks, overlooking how Pentagon leaks expose continuing failures by Congress and the press
| | [Listen to preview](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=114434922&utm_source=podcast-email&play_audio=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTE0NDM0OTIyLCJpYXQiOjE2ODE0Mzc4MDEsImV4cCI6MTY4NDAyOTgwMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.smqxldQ-pZkPioEuUrM6h28UabEp1K659TshDSULKIA&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email#play)|
---|---|---
---
---
[](https://substack.com/redirect/7b72ea38-5d4b-4e5b-bc31-392271ce9e6d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)| | Shahid Buttar| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=114434922&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTE0NDM0OTIyLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTY4MTQzNzgwMSwiZXhwIjoxNjg0MDI5ODAxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.-xdOjZDsohpAJl9jcHUMchoi9fRxqcuwuyfuybzB_Cg&utm_medium=email&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=114434922&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTE0NDM0OTIyLCJpYXQiOjE2ODE0Mzc4MDEsImV4cCI6MTY4NDAyOTgwMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.smqxldQ-pZkPioEuUrM6h28UabEp1K659TshDSULKIA&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=114434922&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTE0NDM0OTIyLCJpYXQiOjE2ODE0Mzc4MDEsImV4cCI6MTY4NDAyOTgwMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.smqxldQ-pZkPioEuUrM6h28UabEp1K659TshDSULKIA)
Last week, a trove of [previously secret Pentagon documents were exposed](https://substack.com/redirect/9b7b3750-1ebf-4845-b9b1-d5f5f0e80e0f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to the world, after having been previously posted on a series of [Discord servers](https://substack.com/redirect/a277b31f-0e5d-472f-8036-1fc833385ec3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) frequented by a community of gamers. Most discussion of the leaks has profoundly mistaken their significance, charitably ignoring the actors who need most to be held to account. By mistaking a tree for the forest, these reports continue to reinforce an illegitimate bipartisan consensus favoring militarism at the cost of democracy, transparency, and human rights.
[A] collective failure of oversight, more than any of the discreet secrets that have been exposed, should be the overarching take away from this saga.
### Red herrings
Most public reports examining the leaks have focused on various military and foreign policy secrets that were exposed. For instance, one document revealed [America's duplicity towards its allies](https://substack.com/redirect/7f815d17-8858-407c-8cb7-2b64149a21c4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) including South Korea. Another suggested that the Israeli [Mossad mobilized supporters](https://substack.com/redirect/8f21c25c-21ac-4d4a-8bb9-5f13d8de6f17?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to challenge Netanyahu's most recent attempts to formalize authoritarianism by sacking the Israeli judiciary. A third confirmed that [Ukrainian munitions are running dangerously low](https://substack.com/redirect/44e15996-f10f-41b6-a02f-f4dec1fd6ecc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), especially anti-aircraft missiles on which the country's ground forces have relied.
Many voices, seemingly unaware of the history of its continuing corruption, uncritically focused on the [supposed costs](https://substack.com/redirect/808e2b03-3dc6-48aa-a5ed-0636605741ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to the U.S. national security establishment. That history, however, reveals that the actual costs are limited, while the constitutional benefits are both massive and widely overlooked.
The intelligence agencies responsible for crafting public lies have been shamed and embarrassed by the exposure of their duplicity in any number of arenas. But the damage to their reputations in no way undermines the national security of We the People of the United States. In fact, it advances the incomparable value of "an alert and knowledgeable citizenry," which President Eisenhower [described](https://substack.com/redirect/cb810e0c-224f-46ac-a96c-4a6130a63023?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as critical to "compel the proper meshing of the huge industrial and military machinery of defense with our peaceful methods and goals, so that security and liberty may prosper together."
Other writers documented the [senseless expansion](https://substack.com/redirect/b0e18bd7-9c73-418c-bea5-8fe6fd3a4820?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of a document classification system through which the national security establishment supposedly limits access to information while ultimately [losing track of its own secrets](https://substack.com/redirect/0b9cb883-e1bc-43eb-8c81-68953456f411?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Previous administrators of the classification system have for years described it as ["bloated" and "dysfunctional."](https://substack.com/redirect/0f10592a-8cd3-4da0-87a6-d695167f1f89?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) When I ran for Congress, reforming it was among the central planks of my platform, which the press generally ignored despite its alignment with their own ethical interests and obligations to the public.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a095dc36-e836-40bf-aa75-eda6824bb7d8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Another red herring included the _Washington Post_ 's publication this week of [a long form expose](https://substack.com/redirect/cbd2d98b-b715-4cf4-a71f-f4235a582ebc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) based on interviews with teen gamers who grew acquainted with the alleged leaker through a Discord server he reportedly administered. A fascinating picture emerges of a right-wing government intelligence operative with a vague critique of "government overreach," an apparently limited grasp of operational security, and a cadre of young admirers who he wanted to impress.
Earlier today, [the FBI arrested Jack Texeira](https://substack.com/redirect/49ae40bf-a6dd-42c8-8688-8eb8222451ee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a member of the intelligence department of the Massachusetts Air National Guard, at his home in North Dighton, Massachusetts. If past is prologue, he will face an array of efforts to discredit him, from attacks on his character (of the sort that Edward Snowden faced) or his sanity, lucidity, and credibility (of the sort that, a generation earlier, drove President Nixon to order a break-in at a psychiatrist's office in the Watergate Hotel in order to discredit [military whistleblower Daniel Ellsberg](https://substack.com/redirect/d2b7250c-81f0-4bc3-83f4-6da9695c215b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)).
While the teen who came to know Texeira distinguishes him from whistleblowers like Snowden & Ellsberg based on their respective motivations, the constitutional functions they have each played (informing the public despite the machinations of bureaucrats) is remarkably similar. In any case, focusing on the leaker--rather than what he revealed--is a classic tactic of intelligence agencies responding to embarrassing leaks.
I write to expose and challenge corruption in the U.S., where journalists have unfortunately made themselves complicit. My reach is insignificant compared to their's, however, so I encourage you to share this post with others who might learn from it.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=114434922&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTE0NDM0OTIyLCJpYXQiOjE2ODE0Mzc4MDEsImV4cCI6MTY4NDAyOTgwMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.smqxldQ-pZkPioEuUrM6h28UabEp1K659TshDSULKIA)
### What journalists have ignored
Lost in seemingly every public report on the leaks is the dire, long-standing, and [continuing failure by Congress to assert its oversight powers](https://substack.com/redirect/0bc9dd00-2cd7-44c6-b726-71bed1da2b2b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Every single one of the secrets revealed in the Pentagon leaks represents a failure of congressional overseers to mind the proverbial store.
In addition to exposing the failures of Congress, the leaks also expose the abject failure of journalists to provide even a modicum of transparency into the continuing military industrial corruption of which [America was warned 60 years ago](https://substack.com/redirect/cb810e0c-224f-46ac-a96c-4a6130a63023?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by the last president elected to the White House as a war hero. The uncritical amplification of Pentagon lies by mass media sources has enabled each and every one of our nation's many wars waged since the Second World War. [The pattern appears to repeat](https://substack.com/redirect/6dfbdcee-4e8b-46a1-a348-4f1cd11cb442?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) itself today.
This is no mere failure to cross a T or dot an I. The Pentagon has [never passed an independent audit](https://substack.com/redirect/6a13e715-f247-4a69-9d5f-f76b4f7a1504?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and in 2018 was [caught after losing track of $21 trillion in missing funds](https://substack.com/redirect/34d95219-f455-4084-9459-91e6ea4a81e8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The failure of Congress to monitor how America's tax dollars have been spent, and the failures of journalists to inform the public about the scope or scale of the Pentagon's ongoing fraud, waste, and abuse has allowed the military--and corporate contractors with their [own private interests](https://substack.com/redirect/33d58a7e-e9e1-445e-9455-7eedefdb84cb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--to pursue any number of fraudulent, deadly adventures.
The Pentagon's scandal du jour in the 80s and 90s involved selling weapons to Iran in order to [run crack cocaine into U.S. cities](https://substack.com/redirect/89563cd3-b883-47c6-b149-2b08e9254f98?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), ultimately enslaving 2.5 million (mostly dark-skinned) Americans through a thoroughly legal process. In the time since then, the CIA has pioneered new human rights abuses in the form of [remote robotic assassinations](https://substack.com/redirect/89a1ad32-a6a7-41f3-86f7-891b5927d14b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), after playing a central role in [abducting hundreds of people seemingly at random, torturing them](https://substack.com/redirect/df3d6e3a-110e-4e6e-9891-9c0eb1d0d376?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (in some cases to death), and detaining them without charge or trial, before [hacking the U.S. Senate](https://substack.com/redirect/f4d93a50-d8c1-4501-9334-82f0b885740b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to hide and steal evidence of its internationally criminal trail.
Three generations ago, a U.S. president called for the CIA to be disbanded. He died in a violent assassination that remains a mystery to this day, largely because official policy continues to protect the secrecy over many of the documents related to JFK's assassination. Why are so many government secrets so widely accepted when so many lies have lurked beneath the surface every time anyone has managed to look?
Rather than suppress the leaks entirely, the press is instead watering them down by reporting on them while reducing their significance to isolated data points, ignoring the pattern they indicate or how it continues to unfold under our noses
In the years since the assassinations of the Kennedy brothers, Martin Luther King, Jr., Fred Hampton, and Malcolm X, the propensity for secrecy in the military establishment has grown only more expansive and pernicious. President Eisenhower warned the public of this tendency, yet every civilian administration since has grown complicit in precisely the pattern that he predicted.
So has Congress, as well as most major news publications.
### On the take
Too many Members of Congress profit from the military industrial complex. That's one reason that Eisenhower's first draft of his famous speech coining the term referred to a "military industrial congressional complex."
Allowing insider trading oligarchs to directly profit from militarism by investing in weapons companies is only the tip of an iceberg. Employees of weapons contractors are prohibited from directly contributing to federal political campaigns, but nothing stops them from bundling campaign contributions or asserting influence through other channels.
Whether indirectly (through bundled political campaign contributions) or directly (through their own shared ownership), too many policymakers share the interests of corporate weapons manufacturers. Their deference to military industrial corruption is not only an abdication of their constitutional responsibilities and oath of office, but also a straightforward defense of their own bank accounts.
Few examples of corruption could rival this pattern. It has driven U.S. foreign policy for the last 75 years, yet few voices in the press have ever publicly recognized it.
I publish on this platform in order to take my writing straight to readers, without the intervention of compromised & co-opted editors. To support my work, please sign up for a free or paid subscription.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..R58_SEpE22-_V2EHirjx5uAcuPqp5Y8UMkmfL0KSQTU?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=114434922)
### Asleep at the switch
The pattern of the Pentagon's lies and obfuscation is disturbingly well-entrenched, revealing that both Congress and the press have repeatedly fallen asleep at the switch.
Every military whistleblower represents a failure of congressional oversight. The reason government employees blow whistles and come forward with information to inform the press is because they encounter information in the course of their jobs that contradicts what their bosses previously claimed in public, usually in congressional hearings before our elected representatives.
Many voices reporting on the latest leaks have noted how classified Pentagon documents were hosted on a nominally public server for several months before anyone noticed. Most of those voices focus their critique on the Pentagon and its [failure to guard its own secrets](https://substack.com/redirect/0b9cb883-e1bc-43eb-8c81-68953456f411?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
But the journalists who repeated the propaganda exposed by the leaks share equal--if not more--blame. Guarding secrets is ancillary to the military's core function, but exposing them is the very purpose of the press.
Yet American journalists routinely defer to their managers in the national security establishment, assisting the executive branch in duping Congress and the voting public again and again. For instance, the _New York Times_ [suppressed](https://substack.com/redirect/b680026b-eb77-457f-b871-14d0dafd1edf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) coverage before the 2004 election of the mass surveillance regime eventually exposed by Edward Snowden in 2013. That editorial decision likely swung a presidential election.
And it was not an isolated incident. Spanning more than half a century, the wars on Vietnam, Iraq, and Afghanistan were all enabled by media sources parroting recurring Pentagon lies.
Even today, the pattern continues in Eastern Europe and elsewhere. Investigative journalist Seymour Hersh has [thoroughly documented](https://substack.com/redirect/6dfbdcee-4e8b-46a1-a348-4f1cd11cb442?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Washington's recent covert attack on the Nordstream pipeline through which Germany used to secure Russian natural gas. Even though the attack represented a prolific act of both international terrorism and ecocide, and Hersh has [a long record](https://substack.com/redirect/639cb092-1ef3-4d46-8eca-8faba9b032c6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of exposing Pentagon crimes that Washington attempted to cover up, most major news publications have refused to cover his most recent findings.
Even as it is being exposed for some of its secrets, the Pentagon continues to somehow hide many of its others in plain sight. Among them is a classified analysis of the [CIA's torture program](https://substack.com/redirect/7c6deb64-7b71-441d-9f00-ada165529cb8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that reportedly documented abuses including the rape and murder of detainees. The recent revelations of Pentagon secrets reflect just a few among God-only-knows how many.
One journalist who demonstrated the temerity to expose the U.S. military's 2007 [assassination of Reuters journalists](https://substack.com/redirect/877c00fd-894a-4e31-9238-9b250c1212e2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Iraq, Julian Assange, is himself the target of a bipartisan prosecution. Another, James Risen, [wrote in 2018](https://substack.com/redirect/8eb1240d-bed7-4c85-8c0c-3b28f93e142b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) about facing government demands in court seeking sources on whom he had relied for his reporting at the _New York Times_ :
> The Obama administration was demanding that I reveal the confidential sources I had relied on for a chapter about a botched CIA operation in my 2006 book, "State of War." I had also written about the CIA operation for the _New York Times_ , but the paper's editors had suppressed the story at the government's request. It wasn't the only time they had done so.
> ...
> My case was part of a broader crackdown on reporters and whistleblowers that had begun during the presidency of George W. Bush and continued far more aggressively under the Obama administration, which had already prosecuted more leak cases than all previous administrations combined. Obama officials seemed determined to use criminal leak investigations to limit reporting on national security. But the crackdown on leaks only applied to low-level dissenters; top officials caught up in leak investigations, like former CIA Director David Petraeus, were still treated with kid gloves.
> ...
> As I took the stand, I thought about how I had ended up here, how much press freedom had been lost, and how drastically the job of national security reporting had changed in the post-9/11 era.
### Retaliation against the public is predictable
As with every supposed threat to U.S. national security, the leaks announced last week will inevitably be used as a pretext to expand mass surveillance. Despite having long monitored social media sites, government "intelligence" agencies somehow overlooked that their secrets were being circulated online for weeks. Resulting calls to [expand government monitoring of social media sites](https://substack.com/redirect/3412ac93-c7bd-4f9e-b805-744eff0ad84b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) are as predictable as the pattern that has repeated itself ever since the authors of the PATRIOT Act opportunistically took advantage of the 9-11 attacks in New York and Washington, D.C.
Beyond a continuing [unaccountable groupthink ](https://substack.com/redirect/2d685a54-5166-4606-9c48-aeb6b5446fd9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)among bureaucrats is a congressional oversight apparatus that is effectively broken, a press corps that is absent or co-opted, and a voting public that is left ignorant as a result.
Ignorance might be bliss for Americans, but our ignorance of Washington's role in the world continues to drive human beings into early graves by the thousands.
### Better 20 years late than never
Another event from last week offers an interesting juxtaposition that has been widely overlooked. On Wednesday, the [Senate approved](https://substack.com/redirect/bb1efed3-c89b-434f-af03-4eb0b0757426?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) a measure to repudiate the 2002 authorization for the use of military force, at the same time that it finally repealed the authorization for the Persian gulf war in 1991.
Each of these votes comes a generation (if not two) after the fact, cementing the abdication by Congress of its warmaking powers to the executive branch.
This, also, is not just a cosmetic failure. At the time when our Republic was founded, the allocation of war making authority was a crucial bone of contention in the drafting of the Constitution. Our founders understood that chief executives share a political incentive to initiate war, and aimed explicitly to prevent it from dragging the country into unnecessary conflict. Writing [The Federalist Number 69](https://substack.com/redirect/16c3bf1b-b576-4fdc-b112-2091de1fcd0a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in 1788, Alexander Hamilton discussed at some length the limited power of the chief executive in the proposed constitutional design, specifically noting that it reserved the power to declare war to the legislature.
That was 150 years before America's emergence as a global hegemon in the wake of the Second World War.
In the time since then, Congress has abandoned its authority to limit the Pentagon, and constrain it from a murderous foreign policy entirely disconnected from the security of We the people of the United States. In doing so, it has effectively invited resource coups around the world, from dozens in Latin America engineered to literally steal fruit to the notorious oil wars of the 2000s.
Will future wars over lithium and increasingly scarce fresh water replace them?
If Congress has anything to say about it, [the answer will be an ignorant and enthusiastic "yes](https://substack.com/redirect/2d685a54-5166-4606-9c48-aeb6b5446fd9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)." And the political consensus supporting that militarism will be enabled by public lies, covered up by the press and supported by the peoples' elected representatives in Washington from both major political parties.
### Here we go again
Washington's [fraudulent proxy conflict](https://substack.com/redirect/6ab69d35-4d8b-4d51-8bd8-074a090f8307?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Ukraine, its foolish and unnecessary escalation in the South China Sea, and recent act of international ecocide and terrorism in the North Atlantic all indicate the continuing unaccountability of a military industrial establishment more dedicated to selling weapons than guarding human rights or keeping people alive.
Voters generally think that their elected voices have priorities more closely aligned with theirs. But their collective failure of oversight, more than any of the discrete secrets that have been exposed, should be the overarching take away from this saga.
Unfortunately, the press is once again doing its best to cover up the crimes of the military industrial establishment. Rather than suppress the leaks entirely, the press is instead watering them down by reporting on them while reducing their significance to isolated data points, ignoring the pattern they indicate or how it continues to unfold under our noses--even now, when increasingly in plain sight.
Some journalists have noted how the leaks expose the bizarre expansiveness of access to top-secret information within the military establishment. This is not news, however. The [overclassification of military secrets](https://substack.com/redirect/0bc9dd00-2cd7-44c6-b726-71bed1da2b2b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) has itself been an open secret for years. I ran for Congress on a platform including proposed reforms to the classification regime in order to force government officials to justify claims of secrecy, and to shift the presumptions under the Freedom of Information Act to better enable the public, press, and policymakers to access information.
Few journalists among the many who covered our campaign understood the profundity of that position or bothered to mention it in their reporting, which tended instead to favor racist disinformation peddled by Democrats across the ideological spectrum loyal to either Pelosi or their own careers. One exception was Jeremy Scahill, an editor at the Intercept who hosted me for [an interview](https://substack.com/redirect/89f3a7ba-f807-49f2-8fe3-025549b9dbff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) three weeks before the character assassination that began on my birthday and ended my career in law and politics. As I explained then:
> I'm gravely disappointed in seeing the Democratic Party line up to promote the militarism that Dr. King warned us to repudiate....[E]very time we've seen voices in the Democratic Party gain widespread traction, it's [when] they oppose militarism. Barack Obama won the White House as the peace candidate and Bernie got a lot of the traction that he got around the country in the last two presidential cycles precisely because he opposes the militarism that unites the corporate wing of the Party.
Transparency is a critical enabler of democracy, without which we cannot claim in any way to respect the consent of the governed. Military secrets, especially in a country with the [largest military budget on Earth](https://substack.com/redirect/06b42159-9959-4bf2-826b-8ec3d595d993?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and global military operations spanning [bases in over 170 countries](https://substack.com/redirect/cdf84b99-c7ee-4eba-93f0-0fe9b4939683?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), are simply incompatible with civilian governance.
They reveal the fascist nature of the United States in 2023, and confirm the historical success of a constitutional coup over the course of the past three generations, as secret agencies have eclipsed Congress as the ultimate decision making authority over how our resources are used across the world.
In this context, the recent leaks are more than simply an embarrassment for the Pentagon. They should be a clarion call for Congress to finally start showing up for work again by doubling down on its oversight responsibilities, tightening purse strings at agencies where corruption appears to have run amok, and insisting on accurate information from government officials from whom members of Congress too often accept [self-serving lies](https://substack.com/redirect/23fced71-3bf3-4297-b6d1-86a211dee24f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Ultimately, the most crucial things for Congress to do now include exposing the rest of the Pentagon's historical secrets, ending the continuing escalation of the conflict in Ukraine, and seeking a diplomatic detente with China. Conflict in neither Europe nor Asia serves American interests, yet the military industrial establishment continues to pound war drums from one side of the planet to the other.
Informed by how often it has been told lies in the past, Congress might, one hopes, develop a spine to restore its own institutional authority, if not the human rights of people around the world abused by the military industrial establishment it has completely failed to check and balance.

Whether indirectly (through bundled political campaign contributions) or directly (through their own shared ownership), too many policymakers share the interests of corporate weapons manufacturers. Their deference to military industrial corruption is not only an abdication of their constitutional responsibilities and oath of office, but also a straightforward defense of their own bank accounts. Few examples of corruption could rival this pattern. It has driven U.S. foreign policy for the last 75 years, yet few voices in the press have ever publicly recognized it. I publish on this platform in order to take my writing straight to readers, without the intervention of compromised & co-opted editors. To support my work, please sign up for a free or paid subscription. Asleep at the switch The pattern of the Pentagon's lies and obfuscation is disturbingly well-entrenched, revealing that both Congress and the press have repeatedly fallen asleep at the switch. Every military whistleblower represents a failure of congressional oversight. The reason government employees blow whistles and come forward with information to inform the press is because they encounter information in the course of their jobs that contradicts what their bosses previously claimed in public, usually in congressional hearings before our elected representatives. Many voices reporting on the latest leaks have noted how classified Pentagon documents were hosted on a nominally public server for several months before anyone noticed. Most of those voices focus their critique on the Pentagon and its . But the journalists who repeated the propaganda exposed by the leaks share equal--if not more--blame. Guarding secrets is ancillary to the military's core function, but exposing them is the very purpose of the press. Yet American journalists routinely defer to their managers in the national security establishment, assisting the executive branch in duping Congress and the voting public again and again. For instance, the New York Times coverage before the 2004 election of the mass surveillance regime eventually exposed by Edward Snowden in 2013. That editorial decision likely swung a presidential election. And it was not an isolated incident. Spanning more than half a century, the wars on Vietnam, Iraq, and Afghanistan were all enabled by media sources parroting recurring Pentagon lies. Even today, the pattern continues in Eastern Europe and elsewhere. Investigative journalist Seymour Hersh has Washington's recent covert attack on the Nordstream pipeline through which Germany used to secure Russian natural gas. Even though the attack represented a prolific act of both international terrorism and ecocide, and Hersh has of exposing Pentagon crimes that Washington attempted to cover up, most major news publications have refused to cover his most recent findings. Even as it is being exposed for some of its secrets, the Pentagon continues to somehow hide many of its others in plain sight. Among them is a classified analysis of the that reportedly documented abuses including the rape and murder of detainees. The recent revelations of Pentagon secrets reflect just a few among God-only-knows how many. One journalist who demonstrated the temerity to expose the U.S. military's 2007 in Iraq, Julian Assange, is himself the target of a bipartisan prosecution. Another, James Risen, about facing government demands in court seeking sources on whom he had relied for his reporting at the New York Times : The Obama administration was demanding that I reveal the confidential sources I had relied on for a chapter about a botched CIA operation in my 2006 book, "State of War." I had also written about the CIA operation for the New York Times , but the paper's editors had suppressed the story at the government's request. It wasn't the only time they had done so. ... My case was part of a broader crackdown on reporters and whistleblowers that had begun during the presidency of George W. Bush and continued far more aggressively under the Obama administration, which had already prosecuted more leak cases than all previous administrations combined. Obama officials seemed determined to use criminal leak investigations to limit reporting on national security. But the crackdown on leaks only applied to low-level dissenters; top officials caught up in leak investigations, like former CIA Director David Petraeus, were still treated with kid gloves. ... As I took the stand, I thought about how I had ended up here, how much press freedom had been lost, and how drastically the job of national security reporting had changed in the post-9/11 era. Retaliation against the public is predictable As with every supposed threat to U.S. national security, the leaks announced last week will inevitably be used as a pretext to expand mass surveillance. Despite having long monitored social media sites, government "intelligence" agencies somehow overlooked that their secrets were being circulated online for weeks. Resulting calls to are as predictable as the pattern that has repeated itself ever since the authors of the PATRIOT Act opportunistically took advantage of the 9-11 attacks in New York and Washington, D.C. Beyond a continuing bureaucrats is a congressional oversight apparatus that is effectively broken, a press corps that is absent or co-opted, and a voting public that is left ignorant as a result. Ignorance might be bliss for Americans, but our ignorance of Washington's role in the world continues to drive human beings into early graves by the thousands. Better 20 years late than never Another event from last week offers an interesting juxtaposition that has been widely overlooked. On Wednesday, the a measure to repudiate the 2002 authorization for the use of military force, at the same time that it finally repealed the authorization for the Persian gulf war in 1991. Each of these votes comes a generation (if not two) after the fact, cementing the abdication by Congress of its warmaking powers to the executive branch. This, also, is not just a cosmetic failure. At the time when our Republic was founded, the allocation of war making authority was a crucial bone of contention in the drafting of the Constitution. Our founders understood that chief executives share a political incentive to initiate war, and aimed explicitly to prevent it from dragging the country into unnecessary conflict. Writing in 1788, Alexander Hamilton discussed at some length the limited power of the chief executive in the proposed constitutional design, specifically noting that it reserved the power to declare war to the legislature. That was 150 years before America's emergence as a global hegemon in the wake of the Second World War. In the time since then, Congress has abandoned its authority to limit the Pentagon, and constrain it from a murderous foreign policy entirely disconnected from the security of We the people of the United States. In doing so, it has effectively invited resource coups around the world, from dozens in Latin America engineered to literally steal fruit to the notorious oil wars of the 2000s. Will future wars over lithium and increasingly scarce fresh water replace them? If Congress has anything to say about it, ." And the political consensus supporting that militarism will be enabled by public lies, covered up by the press and supported by the peoples' elected representatives in Washington from both major political parties. Here we go again Washington's in Ukraine, its foolish and unnecessary escalation in the South China Sea, and recent act of international ecocide and terrorism in the North Atlantic all indicate the continuing unaccountability of a military industrial establishment more dedicated to selling weapons than guarding human rights or keeping people alive. Voters generally think that their elected voices have priorities more closely aligned with theirs. But their collective failure of oversight, more than any of the discrete secrets that have been exposed, should be the overarching take away from this saga. Unfortunately, the press is once again doing its best to cover up the crimes of the military industrial establishment. Rather than suppress the leaks entirely, the press is instead watering them down by reporting on them while reducing their significance to isolated data points, ignoring the pattern they indicate or how it continues to unfold under our noses--even now, when increasingly in plain sight. Some journalists have noted how the leaks expose the bizarre expansiveness of access to top-secret information within the military establishment. This is not news, however. The has itself been an open secret for years. I ran for Congress on a platform including proposed reforms to the classification regime in order to force government officials to justify claims of secrecy, and to shift the presumptions under the Freedom of Information Act to better enable the public, press, and policymakers to access information. Few journalists among the many who covered our campaign understood the profundity of that position or bothered to mention it in their reporting, which tended instead to favor racist disinformation peddled by Democrats across the ideological spectrum loyal to either Pelosi or their own careers. One exception was Jeremy Scahill, an editor at the Intercept who hosted me for three weeks before the character assassination that began on my birthday and ended my career in law and politics. As I explained then: I'm gravely disappointed in seeing the Democratic Party line up to promote the militarism that Dr. King warned us to repudiate....[E]very time we've seen voices in the Democratic Party gain widespread traction, it's [when] they oppose militarism. Barack Obama won the White House as the peace candidate and Bernie got a lot of the traction that he got around the country in the last two presidential cycles precisely because he opposes the militarism that unites the corporate wing of the Party. Transparency is a critical enabler of democracy, without which we cannot claim in any way to respect the consent of the governed. Military secrets, especially in a country with the and global military operations spanning , are simply incompatible with civilian governance. They reveal the fascist nature of the United States in 2023, and confirm the historical success of a constitutional coup over the course of the past three generations, as secret agencies have eclipsed Congress as the ultimate decision making authority over how our resources are used across the world. In this context, the recent leaks are more than simply an embarrassment for the Pentagon. They should be a clarion call for Congress to finally start showing up for work again by doubling down on its oversight responsibilities, tightening purse strings at agencies where corruption appears to have run amok, and insisting on accurate information from government officials from whom members of Congress too often accept . Ultimately, the most crucial things for Congress to do now include exposing the rest of the Pentagon's historical secrets, ending the continuing escalation of the conflict in Ukraine, and seeking a diplomatic detente with China. Conflict in neither Europe nor Asia serves American interests, yet the military industrial establishment continues to pound war drums from one side of the planet to the other. Informed by how often it has been told lies in the past, Congress might, one hopes, develop a spine to restore its own institutional authority, if not the human rights of people around the world abused by the military industrial establishment it has completely failed to check and balance. Which of your friends would learn from this post & podcast? Paid subscribers can access a report I co-authored in 2007 exploring the role of U.S. support for a coup in my native country, Pakistan. Based on dozens of interviews with lawyers, judges, and journalists across the country, it paints a disturbing picture of Washington's complicity in undermining democracy around the world while ironically claiming to support it... name: Mark Meckler, Convention of States Action
subject: How you can help save America!
body:
| | |
---
Fellow Patriot, **[I urge you, right this second, to sign the National Petition for an Article V convention to save America.](http://email.mail.freedompledge.org/c/eJxckc2u1DAMhZ_G3bVKnNz-LLq40NsdbHiAKk3cTlD-aFLQzNOjjgaQ2HhxfGT7fFY-ObvdR-g-AeLNmgyIIN4BcTW9UrJXb7Wjr7F-HOHj6uFnQCz3RH-NrxkNeWXdpXZTZcaBcdWZikbe9lyKgYm36jb2grOB0dppavW2MRLbwFCuvF8Ho6Ws7IgMBZNcMkQheUPayE0PfOtWoxhHkOza02wHkYk-OTI7NfHYKzfeSkn5edQMOOuYm3BqR2dOaqfc6OgBZ6eCsWEHnMthT59uKpQ6F1WodrTb7FSxPwnEfBa_eDL29CCmV7j2EnM8D00gpn-IgnbLi8PLpJVPyu4BxPTMw-urLDrmxdlcloNCUW7Z0h9_DIVCATEF7er08c2f9-77l3fAdgnOGhDTf-LzW2J6DJJlH39Ux2jSL-uczW0Hku1PTDr63wEAAP__teGbcw)** An army of conservative heroes including _**Mark Levin, Ben Shapiro, Sean Hannity**_ , and _**Lt. Col. Allen West**_ have joined our movement to call for an Article V convention. **[Just click here to add your name to this national petition. And then PLEASE forward this email to every single person you know.](http://email.mail.freedompledge.org/c/eJxckb2u2zAMhZ-G3mxIlBLHg4e2rsduHToZjEQ7AvR3LbnFvU9fOEhboAuHwwOS5yOF7N36PkL_GRAfzhZABPUJEO_2RqRvdGk9f0vtxx6_nj38Aoj1PfNf42tGx4GcP9V-auw4CEm9bXiU15vUahDq0jxGw8PQX4ZVCdIClbLWykGwNFfSvbbUuBEFKqGlFohKy46N1asZ5NrfLQmJoMW5p1t3ZptC9mw37tK-NX581JrL86gZcDapdPEwno-SaePSmRQAZ0_RurgBznV3R8gPirUtlSq3njdXPFX3k0HNRw1LYOuOAGp6hbueYknHbhjU9A9RNH55cXiZDIVMbougpmce2Z5lMaks3pW67Bwr-WXNf_wpVo4V1BSNb9f7Yd54u3z_AXhdoncW1PSf-PyWmj4GLUpIb80-2vzLee_KtQctticmk8LvAAAA__-E9Juc)**
Our nation is spiraling out of control. Debt threatens to cripple our economy. Federal spending has never been higher -- nor has our deficit. And our leaders in Washington won't do anything to save us._In fact, most of them are part of the problem!_ An Article V convention is our only hope. You see, our Founding Fathers were brilliant men. They left an emergency clause in the Constitution in case the federal government got too big and out-of-control. **Article V of the Constitution allows the states to bypass Congress and the President to call a convention to propose amendments to our Constitution that will rein in the federal government.** Well, conservative leaders all across the country — along with over _**FIVE MILLION**_ grassroots patriots — believe it is time to invoke Article V and call a convention for the express, _limited_ purpose of imposing fiscal restraints and term limits on Congress and limiting the power, scope, and jurisdiction of the federal government. And I hope you do, too. With the socialists in control in Washington, it's time to take action before it's too late! It's time for "We the People" to use the most powerful weapon in our arsenal - the Constitution - to fight back against Washington's overreach. **[Please sign the National Petition for an Article V convention right now, and please ask everyone you know to do the same.](http://email.mail.freedompledge.org/c/eJxckb2O3DAMhJ-G7mxIlPxXuEjiuAlwRbpUhlaivQL0F0u-y93TB15sEiANi-GA5HxUPjm7vU_QfwbEuzUZEEF8AsSbGZSSg2prRy-x_jjC16uHXwCxvCf6a3zOaMgr6y61nyszjYyr3lQ08W7gUoxMtNV9YrzfhrYV1I1cs65FQZLfjFDjyNTAWWUnZCiY5JIhCskb0kZueuRbfzOKcQTJrj3NdhCZ6JMjs1MTj71y072UlB9HLYCLjrkJp3Z05qR2yo2OHnBxKhgbdsClHPb06a5CqXNRhWpHu81OFftKIJaz-NWTsacHMT_DdZeY43loAjH_QxS0W58cniatfFJ2DyDmRx5eX2XVMa_O5rIeFIpy65b--GMoFAqIOWhX_6Bvy9vLr1f_HbBbg7MGxPyf-PiWmD9GybKPP6tjMunNOmdz14Nk-wOTjv53AAAA__9xh5ra)** Here at Convention of States Action, we are working overtime to convince the required 34 state legislatures to call for an Article V convention to place limits on the power and jurisdiction of the federal government. **We have already passed in 19 of those states — bringing us over HALFWAY to calling a Convention of States and taking the power back from the “ruling class” in Washington!** In 2022, we saw huge victories in Wisconsin, Nebraska, West Virginia, and South Carolina all passing the Convention of States (COS) Resolution. The legislative sessions in many states have started for this year and our grassroots army has been right there, pushing our Resolution through in this General Assembly! NOW is our window of opportunity to double down and secure the needed victories to cross the finish line!
We have a citizen activist army operating in all 50 states, working tirelessly on the front lines to urge their state legislators to act in accordance with the will of the people and demand that they pass this critically important Resolution. **An Article V convention is our only chance to pass important and long overdue measures like a balanced budget amendment, term limits on Congress and the Supreme Court, and limits on the scope and jurisdiction of the federal government.** The politicians in Washington wouldn't dare limit their own power. Therefore, we the people need to do it! But in order for that to happen, we need millions and millions of patriotic Americans like you to sign this important national petition. We need to show the members of the state legislatures that the American people are ready for a constitutional solution as big as our nation's problems, an Article V convention. [**Simply click here to sign and we’ll deliver it directly to your state legislators, free of charge. This is our best chance to save America from an out-of-control federal government.**](http://email.mail.freedompledge.org/c/eJxckT_PnTwMxT-N2UDBCRcYGN63lLHq1KEL8k0MN2r-lYRWz_PpK65uW6mLh-Mj2-dn8snZ7W2C_n9AfFiTARHkf4B4NwORGqirHX-K9fsRPl49_ACI5S3xH-NrRsOerLvUfq7MNIqWelPx1N6GVslRyK56TEQjDsTCyE7qrhsGkkYR90NPqO7tWNkJBUqhWiUQpWob1kZtemy3_m5ItAhKXHua7WA20SfHZucmHnvlpkcpKT-PWgAXHXMTTu34zIl2zo2OHnBxFIwNO-BSDnv69KBQ6lyocO14t9lRsT8Y5HIWv3o29vQg51e42yXmeB6aQc5_EQXt1heHl0mTT2T3AHJ-5mnrq6w65tXZXNaDQyG3bum3P4bCoYCcg3b18vXuv335jMsAeFuDswbk_I_4_Jac30clso_fq2My6ad1zuZbD0rsT0w6-l8BAAD__0plmn8) Washington won't fix itself. The federal government is too big, and our leaders are too invested in its growth. We the People must take control through our state legislatures. [**Please join the fight to call an Article V convention. Sign the petition today, and urge every freedom-loving American to do the same.**](http://email.mail.freedompledge.org/c/eJxckc2unDAMhZ_G7EDByQCzYNGW0l2lPgEyiWFS5a8ktLr36StG01bqxovjI9vnM_nk7PY2Qv8REB_WZEAE-QEQVzMQqYFuteOvsX4_wuerh58Asbwl_mt8zWjYk3WX2k-VGe-ipd5UPLbd0Cp5F_JWPUYp2k0O3BP1ClfVd8g4rKxUx-umCCs7okApVKsEolRtw9qoTd_brV8NiRZBiWtPsx3MJvrk2OzcxGOv3PgoJeXnUTPgrGNuwqkdnznRzrnR0QPOjoKxYQecy2FPnx4USp0LFa4d7zY7KvYng5zP4hfPxp4e5PQK111ijuehGeT0D1HQbnlxeJk0-UR2DyCnZ562vsqiY16czWU5OBRyy5b--GMoHArIKWhX92mg77ekv3wD7JbgrAE5_Sc-vyWn97sS2ccf1TGa9Ms6Z3PXgxL7E5OO_ncAAAD__0Semq4) Thank you, and may God bless America! For America, Mark Meckler President Convention of States Action
[Unsubscribe here](http://email.mail.freedompledge.org/c/eJw8kMFuwyAMQL_GuRWBoQEOHLZ1HPcPEKBFIoGFRFP79VOrbNdn6_nJbm4lp7sB-Q6Itxw6IAJ_A0QflHNCufOpxK96eqzL53OGH4C43Vv8XzwcJM4ulyeVlyEYTZmTYYiGjYoJrik_DzejpNcJtUw6hcBGVNxzOqbx7BAVcj9kgxQ5FUxQRC4YiVMQadIsSR8cZQiCPu-QtMYY6txKDNdI6nodirltW-uvKAtoW-9k2acS9_5KI1OdAe2RC2j77vu05rblunRAuy8H8RG4fT2DXx5a0D7X72E1of3kUnIfJQh6_VP-BgAA__-PdGPB)
